title: Polymarket API Introduction
summary: Introduction to Polymarket APIs and service boundaries across market data and trading.
actionable_lessons: Separate market-data, Gamma, and trading assumptions when building retrieval or execution workflows.
Use this as the canonical reference for API scope and integration boundaries.
anti_misuse_notes: An API introduction does not define exchange-state edge cases by itself.
Do not infer latency or fill quality from interface descriptions.

title: Polymarket WebSocket Overview
summary: Primary documentation for streaming Polymarket market data over WebSockets.
actionable_lessons: Use the official stream model when reasoning about signal freshness and market-state observability.
Ground reconnect and stale-data handling in the actual WebSocket contract.
anti_misuse_notes: Streaming availability does not imply the local bot is consuming every event correctly.
This does not justify assuming no data loss.

title: Polymarket Orderbook
summary: Official documentation for orderbook behavior and trading primitives.
actionable_lessons: Use orderbook definitions to evaluate midpoint, best bid/ask, and safe price-cap logic.
Map execution assumptions to actual book mechanics before relaxing guards.
anti_misuse_notes: Orderbook semantics do not prove you can fill at midpoint.
Do not treat book snapshots as guaranteed executable prices.

title: Polymarket Matching Engine
summary: Official description of matching-engine behavior, including restart-related edge cases.
actionable_lessons: Use restart and exchange-state guidance to build bounded retries and safer execution fallback handling.
Treat exchange availability and restart windows as first-class execution risks.
anti_misuse_notes: Matching-engine docs do not remove the need for local audit and reconciliation.
Do not assume retries are always safe without time and price checks.

title: Polymarket Error Codes
summary: Official reference for Polymarket API and trading error codes.
actionable_lessons: Map runtime order failures to documented error classes before changing retry behavior.
Use explicit error-code handling in execution telemetry and memo generation.
anti_misuse_notes: Error-code documentation is not a substitute for local failure attribution.
Do not treat undocumented errors as safe to ignore.

title: Binance Spot API Documentation
summary: Official Binance Spot API repository covering REST and WebSocket market-data contracts.
actionable_lessons: Ground signal-feed parsing and stream assumptions in the official Binance schema.
Use this source to validate delta windows, stream payloads, and reconnect semantics.
anti_misuse_notes: This does not prove Binance momentum predicts Polymarket resolution.
Do not confuse clean feed integration with alpha.

title: Binance Public Data
summary: Official Binance repository for historical market data snapshots and download conventions.
actionable_lessons: Use official historical data for replay validation and feed sanity checks.
Benchmark local feed reconstruction against exchange-published history.
anti_misuse_notes: Historical Binance data alone does not model Polymarket execution or oracle resolution.
Do not use this as a direct profitability proof.

title: Chainlink Developer Documentation
summary: Primary Chainlink developer documentation for feeds, oracle architecture, and integration behavior.
actionable_lessons: Use Chainlink docs to reason about oracle source structure, update behavior, and trust boundaries.
Ground resolution-source assumptions in Chainlink documentation rather than exchange folklore.
anti_misuse_notes: Oracle documentation does not guarantee exact market-resolution timing in Polymarket.
Do not assume a Binance move immediately maps to oracle resolution.

title: Chainlink Data Feeds Directory
summary: Official directory of Chainlink feeds and feed metadata.
actionable_lessons: Use feed metadata to understand heartbeat and deviation assumptions around the BTC/USD feed.
Treat feed freshness and feed identity as explicit runtime dependencies.
anti_misuse_notes: Feed metadata is not a direct predictor of market direction.
Do not use this to justify aggressive latency claims.

title: A New Interpretation of Information Rate
summary: John Kelly's original paper linking edge and growth-optimal capital allocation.
actionable_lessons: Sizing should be a function of edge quality and uncertainty, not confidence alone.
Small bet sizes reduce ruin risk when edge is unproven.
anti_misuse_notes: Kelly sizing is dangerous when the edge estimate is noisy.
Do not use Kelly to justify live risk before the strategy is validated.

title: The Kelly Criterion in Blackjack, Sports Betting, and the Stock Market
summary: Edward Thorp's practitioner reference on applying Kelly-style sizing under uncertainty.
actionable_lessons: Use fractional sizing and survival-first logic when edge and variance are not stable.
Treat bankroll preservation as a first-order objective.
anti_misuse_notes: This does not justify running an unproven strategy live just because size is small.
Sizing discipline cannot rescue a negative expectancy process.

title: Prediction Markets in Theory and Practice
summary: Foundational paper on how prediction markets aggregate information and where they can fail.
actionable_lessons: Use prediction-market theory to reason about information aggregation, not just price momentum.
Separate market mechanics from forecasting quality.
anti_misuse_notes: Prediction markets can still be noisy, illiquid, and microstructure-distorted.
Do not assume market price is perfectly calibrated.

title: High-frequency Trading in a Limit Order Book
summary: Canonical paper on high-frequency execution, inventory risk, and quoting in limit-order-book markets.
actionable_lessons: Execution quality and inventory control are part of the strategy, not separate concerns.
Model late-entry and adverse-selection risk explicitly.
anti_misuse_notes: This paper is not a blueprint for blindly market making in Polymarket.
Do not import continuous-market assumptions without adaptation.

title: Simulating and Analyzing Order Book Data: The Queue-Reactive Model
summary: Order-book simulation framework focused on queue dynamics and local market-state evolution.
actionable_lessons: Use execution-aware simulation instead of midpoint-only replay when judging strategy viability.
Model queue and fill uncertainty directly.
anti_misuse_notes: This does not mean a full LOB simulator is required before every decision.
Do not overfit on simulated microstructure without validating against real fills.

title: Flow Toxicity and Liquidity in a High-frequency World
summary: Paper on adverse selection, toxic order flow, and liquidity quality in high-frequency markets.
actionable_lessons: Treat late, expensive entries as potentially toxic flow rather than 'high confidence'.
Use this lens when deciding whether to relax price and timing guards.
anti_misuse_notes: A toxicity model does not automatically produce profitable trades.
Do not treat a high win rate at bad prices as a safe live edge.

title: DeepLOB: Deep Convolutional Neural Networks for Limit Order Books
summary: Research on learning predictive structure from limit-order-book states with deep models.
actionable_lessons: Feature engineering should preserve short-horizon market-state context, not just scalar deltas.
Model input quality matters as much as model class.
anti_misuse_notes: Do not jump to deep learning without sufficient clean labeled data.
This does not justify black-box live trading.

title: A Century of Evidence on Trend-Following Investing
summary: Practitioner research on long-run evidence for trend-following and robust research discipline.
actionable_lessons: Trend-following edges should be judged over long out-of-sample windows, not a few attractive sessions.
A strong research culture emphasizes robustness over story-telling.
anti_misuse_notes: This does not validate a 5-minute crypto prediction-market strategy by analogy.
Do not import broad futures results directly into Polymarket.

title: Probability Calibration
summary: Official documentation on calibration curves, calibrated probabilities, and evaluation of predictive probability quality.
actionable_lessons: Judge signal quality using calibration, not only hit rate.
Use calibrated probability thinking when converting signals into trade decisions.
anti_misuse_notes: Calibration metrics do not replace execution and cost modeling.
Do not equate a calibrated predictor with a profitable strategy automatically.

title: Deflating the Sharpe Ratio
summary: Lopez de Prado's work on correcting Sharpe ratios for multiple testing and backtest selection bias.
actionable_lessons: Do not promote strategies based on the best backtest in a large search space without adjustment.
Penalize promising replay results for selection bias before trusting them.
anti_misuse_notes: A corrected Sharpe still does not prove live execution will work.
Do not use this paper as an excuse to ignore simpler gate blockers like audit mismatch or negative live P&L.

title: Optimal Trading Rules Without Backtesting
summary: Lopez de Prado's paper on avoiding parameter overfitting when selecting trading rules.
actionable_lessons: Use explicit rule-selection discipline instead of iterating until a replay looks good.
Bound the search space and record hypotheses when changing live gating logic.
anti_misuse_notes: This does not replace execution-aware testing.
Do not confuse reduced overfitting risk with proven profitability.
